response.justification: Sarah has a broad polyglot background with genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, which is the most relevant signal on the resume. However, the role demands deep, production-grade C++ or Rust expertise as its primary requirement, and Sarah's C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project — neither indicative of the commercial-grade systems programming the role requires. Her career trajectory has moved firmly toward TypeScript/Node.js web and platform engineering, not low-level systems or routing pipeline work. The GIS experience, while real, is oriented toward NGO data collection rather than routing tile generation, traffic signal integration, or navigation accuracy at scale.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform experience via GISCollective and Green Map, demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts and open-source GIS ecosystems
Demonstrated ability to operate in distributed, high-throughput environments (Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency DLang)
Strong software craftsmanship ethos (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a track record of building and shipping production systems across multiple domains
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old (NI LabWindows 2014–2015, thesis project); no Rust experience mentioned anywhere on the resume
No experience with routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or traffic-data integration — the core technical domain of the role
Primary engineering identity is TypeScript/Node.js full-stack; the career arc since 2015 shows no movement toward systems-level or geospatial pipeline work
